ddls:
CREATE TABLE "network_devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the network device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the network device (e.g., router, switch) */,
  "revision" TEXT /* Revision number of the device */,
  "max_instances" TEXT /* Maximum number of instances allowed for the device */,
  "vendor" TEXT /* Vendor of the network device */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the network device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the network device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance performed on the device */,
  "current_instances" TEXT /* Current number of instances in operation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_instances" (
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device instance */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network device this instance belongs to */,
  "instance_number" TEXT /* Instance number of the device */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the instance (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "entry_port" TEXT /* Entry port number for the instance */,
  "exit_port" TEXT /* Exit port number for the instance */,
  "current_bandwidth_usage" REAL /* Current bandwidth usage in Gbps */,
  "max_bandwidth" REAL /* Maximum allowed bandwidth in Gbps */,
  "vlan_id" TEXT /* VLAN ID assigned to the instance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("instance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_instances_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ports" (
  "port_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each port */,
  "instance_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device instance this port belongs to */,
  "port_number" TEXT /* Port number on the device instance */,
  "port_status" TEXT /* Current status of the port (e.g., open, closed) */,
  "port_type" TEXT /* Type of the port (e.g., Ethernet, Fiber) */,
  "physical_address" TEXT /* Physical address (MAC) of the port */,
  "bandwidth" REAL /* Bandwidth capacity of the port in Gbps */,
  "duplex_mode" TEXT /* Duplex mode of the port (e.g., full-duplex, half-duplex) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("port_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ports_instance_id FOREIGN KEY ("instance_id") REFERENCES device_instances ("instance_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_attributes" (
  "attribute_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each attribute */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network device this attribute belongs to */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the attribute */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the attribute (e.g., UINT, Array of Struct) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the attribute */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value for the attribute */,
  "current_value" TEXT /* Current value of the attribute */,
  "is_mandatory" INTEGER /* Whether the attribute is mandatory */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attribute_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_attributes_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the device associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., status change, performance issue) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed alert message */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "is_acknowledged" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been acknowledged by a user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES network_devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, admin) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken by the user */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Detailed information about the action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
